description: Backend: Local Media Library and Branding system - routers, services, models, and LocalMediaProvider
steps: Step 1: Add new Pydantic models to backend/app/models/schemas.py: LibraryItem (id, filename, original_filename, category: 'music'|'image'|'video', labels: list[str], description: str, file_path: str, created_at: str, file_size: int), LibraryCategory enum, BrandingConfig (id, image_path, position: 'top-left'|'top-right'|'bottom-left'|'bottom-right'|'top-center'|'bottom-center', size_percent: float 10-50, opacity: float 0.1-1.0, enabled: bool), BrandingPosition enum. Also add branding_config optional field to AssembleVideoRequest and GenerateFullRequest.

Step 2: Create backend/app/services/library_service.py - a service that manages the local media library using JSON metadata file at output/library/metadata.json. Methods: add_item(file, category, labels, description) -> LibraryItem, list_items(category?, labels?) -> list[LibraryItem], get_item(item_id) -> LibraryItem, delete_item(item_id) -> bool, search_items(query: str) -> list[LibraryItem] (matches query against labels and description). Store files in output/library/music/, output/library/images/, output/library/videos/ based on category.

Step 3: Create backend/app/services/branding_service.py - manages branding config stored in output/branding/config.json. Methods: upload_branding(file) -> BrandingConfig, get_config() -> BrandingConfig|None, update_config(position, size_percent, opacity, enabled) -> BrandingConfig, delete_branding() -> bool. Store uploaded branding images in output/branding/.

Step 4: Create backend/app/routers/library.py - REST endpoints: POST /api/library/upload (multipart form: file, category, labels comma-separated, description), GET /api/library (query params: category, labels, search), GET /api/library/{item_id}, DELETE /api/library/{item_id}, GET /api/library/search?query=... Register router in main.py.

Step 5: Create backend/app/routers/branding.py - REST endpoints: POST /api/branding/upload (multipart form: file, position, size_percent, opacity), GET /api/branding, PUT /api/branding (JSON body: position, size_percent, opacity, enabled), DELETE /api/branding. Register router in main.py.

Step 6: Create LocalMediaProvider class in backend/app/services/local_media_provider.py. Subclass MediaProvider from media_sourcer.py. The search method loads library metadata and matches the query keywords against item labels using simple substring/word matching. Returns MediaItems with local_path already set (no download needed). is_configured() returns True if output/library/metadata.json exists and has items. Set name='local_library', supported_media_types=[MediaType.video, MediaType.image].
Step 7: Register LocalMediaProvider FIRST in the provider_registry in media_sourcer.py (insert at position 0 before PexelsProvider). This ensures the local library is checked before any external API.

Step 8: Update video_assembler.py assemble_video() to accept an optional branding_config parameter (BrandingConfig). If branding is enabled and an image exists, load the branding image as an ImageClip, resize to size_percent of video width, set opacity, and position it using CompositeVideoClip on top of each scene clip (or on the final concatenated video). Use MoviePy's CompositeVideoClip to overlay the branding.

Step 9: Update backend/app/routers/videos.py assemble endpoint and generate-full endpoint to pass branding_config through to assemble_video. Load branding config from branding_service if not explicitly provided in the request.

Step 10: Mount static file serving for library files in main.py startup: mount /static/library pointing to output/library/ directory so frontend can preview uploaded media. Also mount /static/branding for branding images.
Step 11: Add python-multipart to backend dependencies in pyproject.toml (needed for file upload endpoints).
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/library/upload accepts a file with metadata and stores it in the correct category directory
GET /api/library returns all items, filterable by category and labels
GET /api/library/search?query=nature returns items whose labels contain 'nature'
DELETE /api/library/{item_id} removes the item and its file
POST /api/branding/upload stores a branding image and returns config
GET /api/branding returns current branding config or null
LocalMediaProvider is registered first in provider_registry and returns local items when labels match
Video assembler applies branding overlay when config is present
Backend starts without errors with all new routers registered
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/abetVideos/backend && pip install -e '.[dev]' && python -c 'from app.main import app; print("Backend loads OK")'
cd /projects/sandbox/abetVideos/backend && python -m pytest tests/ -x 2>/dev/null; echo 'Tests checked'

cd /projects/sandbox/abetVideos/backend && python -c 'from app.services.media_sourcer import provider_registry; providers = provider_registry.get_all_providers(); assert providers[0].name == "local_library", f"First provider is {providers[0].name}"; print("LocalMediaProvider is first")'
